searchProcess.robotsOrAccessNotes: Domain accessible; WebFetch returned clean content; no bot block on international medical education hub.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: International Visiting Medical Students | International Medical Education
candidateFindings.shortQuote: You must be in the final year of training at your home school or you must have completed one year of core clerkship rotations. You must be actively enrolled in a program leading to the equivalent of an MD or DO.
candidateFindings.specialtyDecision: Multispecialty per catalog (Surgery, Pediatrics, Neurology, IM and others enumerated)

candidateFindings.pageTitle: International Visiting Medical Students | International Medical Education
candidateFindings.shortQuote: The application fee of $350 is non-refundable. Cost per 4-week non-credit elective is $5,200. This includes tuition, housing and transport to and from the airport.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: International Visiting Medical Students | International Medical Education
candidateFindings.shortQuote: students participating in the IVMS Program enter the United States on a B-1/B-2 Visa with passport stamp for B-1 (Business)

negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Page does not enumerate every specialty available; catalog must be consulted for specialty-level availability (separate course catalog URL exists at apps.medicine.uab.edu/catalog).

nextAction: Eligible for future public-safe activation with caveats. If activated, must carry: (a) B-1/B-2 visa requirement, (b) TOEFL iBT 100 (22 speaking) requirement, (c) MS-final-year-or-post-1yr-core-clerkships restriction, (d) committee-reviewed (not guaranteed) language, (e) $5,200/4wk + $350 application fee verbatim, (f) housing-included note. Separate IMG-graduate observership lane also exists at /observership URL — that needs its own packet in a future sprint.
plainEnglishSummary: UAB in Birmingham runs a real International Visiting Medical Students program for international medical students in their final year (or post-1-year-core-clerkships). The cost is $5,200 per 4-week rotation plus a $350 non-refundable application fee, housing included. Students enter on a B-1/B-2 visa, must score 100 on TOEFL iBT with at least 22 on speaking, and applications are committee-reviewed not guaranteed. Max 8 weeks total.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Alabama gained a verified international-student-eligible USCE pathway with verbatim cost + visa + duration evidence; no noindex/backend/schema work touched.
